operational_definition: A gap candidate is a deterministic title n-gram concept pair where both concepts are frequent in the cutoff-before corpus, both are active in the recent pre-cutoff window, concept tokens do not overlap, and historical co-occurrence is <= max_past_cooccur. Candidate selection uses only pre-cutoff counts; future documents are used only for evaluation.
